sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, time_col TIME, dist_col INT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (time_col, dist_col) VALUES (time '12:00:00', 2), (time '14:30:00', 3), (time '16:45:00', 3), (time '18:00:00', NULL), (time '20:15:00', 1);
SELECT count(distinct dist_col) FROM test_table WHERE dist_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT time_col, count(distinct dist_col) FROM test_table GROUP BY time_col HAVING time_col < time '20:00:00';
SELECT count(distinct dist_col) FROM (SELECT time '18:00:00' as time_val, dist_col FROM test_table WHERE dist_col IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;
SELECT count(distinct dist_col) FROM test_table WHERE dist_col IN (SELECT distinct dist_col FROM test_table WHERE time_col >= time '12:00:00' AND time_col <= time '16:00:00');
SELECT count(distinct dist_col) FROM (SELECT time_col, dist_col FROM test_table WHERE time_col IS NOT NULL) AS subquery HAVING count(distinct dist_col) > 1;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, time_col TIME, ref_id INT REFERENCES test_table(id));
SELECT count(distinct a.ref_id) FROM another_table a JOIN test_table t ON a.ref_id = t.id WHERE t.dist_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT time_col, count(distinct dist_col) AS distinct_count FROM test_table GROUP BY time_col ORDER BY time_col DESC;
